factors.summary: Cost reduction programs and integration efforts have successfully delivered measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion. However, the decrease in accrued compensation was partially offset by current year accruals.

evidence.text: Cost management and integration efforts are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion.
As a percentage of revenue, Adjusted EBITDA continued to improve due to margin expansion from revenue growth and continued integration and business leveraging efforts.
The decrease in accrued compensation was primarily due to the 2017 bonus payout made in the first quarter of 2018, partially offset by the current year accrual.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful cost reduction and margin expansion.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving and expanding, driven by the business model's leverage and cost management efforts. The improvement is evidenced by a 12% year-over-year increase in Adjusted EBITDA margin.

evidence.text: Adjusted EBITDA increased primarily due to the leverage in our business model, as incremental revenue drives higher margins. We also continue to focus our efforts on cost management to improve overall margins.

Our Adjusted EBITDA margin performance for the three months ended February 28, 2018 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2017 , increased primarily because of margin flow-through on our organic revenue growth, as well as our continued integration and cost management efforts.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are improving, driven by business model leverage and cost management.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive organic revenue growth across all segments, with notable strength in Transportation and Financial Services. Additionally, the stable organic revenue growth is supporting improved EBITDA margins through margin flow-through and cost management efforts.

evidence.text: We saw broad-based organic revenue growth across all four of our segments for the three months ended February 28, 2018, compared to the three months ended February 28, 2017, with particular strength in Transportation and Financial Services and improving performance in Resources and CMS.

Our Adjusted EBITDA margin performance for the three months ended February 28, 2018 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2017 , increased primarily because of margin flow-through on our organic revenue growth, as well as our continued integration and cost management efforts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Company is experiencing positive organic revenue growth across all segments with notable strength in key areas.

factors.summary: The company is significantly affected by foreign currency movements, particularly impacting its revenue through fluctuations in the British Pound, Euro, Canadian Dollar, Singapore Dollar, and Indian Rupee. Additionally, foreign exchange movements and economic conditions negatively impacted the company's EBITDA expansion, leading to higher revenue and expense amounts as well as lower margins.

evidence.text: Our largest foreign currency exposures are the British Pound, Euro, Canadian Dollar, Singapore Dollar, and Indian Rupee.
The expansion was negatively impacted by changes in foreign currency exchange rates, which resulted in higher revenue and expense amounts, as well as low aM margins.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Company faces significant foreign currency impacts negatively affecting margins and EBITDA expansion.
